prompts.label: Create React app with Vite
prompts.text:
I'm a beginner with React. Guide me to create a project with Vite + React + TypeScript.

- Exact terminal commands
- Explain src/App.tsx, main.tsx, index.html
- Replace default content with a heading Hello React
- How to run dev server and open in browser

List common errors (port in use) and fixes in one line each.

prompts.label: Component with props
prompts.text:
Teach React props with a simple example (TypeScript if possible).

- Greeting component: props { name: string; age?: number }
- App renders 2–3 Greeting instances
- Explain props are read-only

One file per component or single App.tsx for simplicity — your choice, but explain structure.

prompts.label: Counter with useState
prompts.text:
Build a counter component using useState.

- Display count, buttons +1, -1, Reset
- Explain useState syntax and re-render in plain language
- Optional: set minimum 0

Provide full App.tsx (or Counter.tsx + App).

prompts.label: Render a list with keys
Controlled form input
prompts.text:
Create a controlled form in React for beginners.

- Input for name, submit button
- On submit: preventDefault, alert or show message below (no backend)
- Store value in useState

Explain controlled vs uncontrolled in 2 sentences.

prompts.label: useEffect + fetch data
prompts.text:
Fetch data in React with useEffect (beginner level).

- On mount, fetch https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1
- Show loading, error, and data states
- Type the user shape with TypeScript interface

Warn about missing dependency array mistakes briefly.

prompts.label: Conditional rendering
prompts.text:
Teach conditional rendering in React.

- Boolean isLoggedIn in state, toggle button
- Show different JSX when true/false
- Use both && pattern and ternary once each

Keep UI minimal, no router.

prompts.label: Custom hook useCounter
prompts.text:
Introduce a custom hook useCounter(initial).

- Returns { count, increment, decrement, reset }
- Use it in App.tsx
- Explain rules of hooks in 3 bullets

TypeScript preferred.

prompts.label: Two pages with React Router
prompts.text:
Add React Router to a small Vite React app (beginner).

- Install react-router-dom
- Routes: / home, /about about page
- Link navigation in a header
- Explain BrowserRouter and Routes structure

List install command and which files to create.

prompts.label: Theme context (light/dark)
prompts.text:
Show React Context API with a simple theme toggle.

- ThemeContext: 'light' | 'dark'
- Provider at top, button in child to toggle
- Apply a CSS class or inline style on a wrapper

Explain when Context is overkill vs props.

prompts.label: React.memo pragmatic rules
prompts.text:
Explain memo at a beginner level with props reference equality.
Demonstrate when wrapping a purely presentational list item helps versus hurts readability.
Connect to stable callback pitfalls with inline functions.
Show React DevTools highlight updates as a debugging tip.
Conclude with a heuristic: memo after measuring, not by default.

prompts.label: useReducer for form wizard
prompts.text:
Replace several useState calls with useReducer for a 3-step checkout wizard.
Model actions like NEXT, BACK, SET_SHIPPING, RESET.
Show how to keep related state cohesive.
Compare mental overhead to useState for learners.
Include a tiny reducer with exhaustive switch and default guard.

prompts.label: useRef focus management
prompts.text:
Build a dialog that moves focus to the first input on open and returns focus to the trigger on close.
Use useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null) and optional useEffect timing notes.
Mention tab traps only as a follow-up reading pointer.
Keep the example dependency-light.
Add keyboard Escape handling hook stub.

prompts.label: Error boundary class component
prompts.text:
I only know function components. Teach the minimal class-based ErrorBoundary pattern.
Show componentDidCatch logging and getDerivedStateFromError.
Explain what boundaries cannot catch (event handlers, async).
Wire a simple fallback UI with retry button pattern.
Note React 19 future directions only lightly without derailing.

prompts.label: useLayoutEffect for measured DOM
prompts.text:
Contrast useEffect vs useLayoutEffect with a tooltip positioning example.
Explain why useLayoutEffect can reduce visual flicker.
Warn about SSR considerations and suppressHydrationWarning tangents briefly.
Give a rule of thumb on when beginners should still default to useEffect.
Include a dev-only performance note.

prompts.label: createPortal for modals
prompts.text:
Show ReactDOM.createPortal rendering a modal into document.body.
Explain why this helps with z-index and clipped parents.
Discuss event bubbling through the React tree vs DOM tree at a high level.
Pair with a simple focus outline style.
List one accessibility follow-up (aria-modal) as homework.

prompts.label: Keys beyond id: composition pitfalls
prompts.text:
Deepen my understanding of key when list items lack database ids yet.
Explain why index-as-key is risky with reordering or insertion.
Show generating composite keys carefully.
Relate to deduplication in React reconciliation without deep internals.
Give a quick exercise: sortable list bug story.

prompts.label: forwardRef on design-system button
prompts.text:
Teach forwardRef so a parent can focus my <Button>.
Include TypeScript generic props pattern at a friendly level.
Set displayName for devtools.
Contrast with callback ref pattern.
Mention ref forwarding limitations on host components vs wrappers.

prompts.label: useId for server-friendly form wiring
prompts.text:
Replace Math.random() keys in forms with useId for linking labels and inputs.
Explain hydration mismatch risks useId avoids.
Show pairing with aria-describedby for helper text.
Note multiple ids via suffix pattern.
Keep example in a small component only.

prompts.label: Deferred value for search suggestions
prompts.text:
Introduce useDeferredValue for keeping input snappy while a heavy filtered list updates.
Contrast with debouncing in userland.
Explain perceived lag trade-offs simply.
Show a toy list filter example without external libraries.
Mention profiling in React DevTools as validation.
